table_description: Insurance Agencies and Brokers. Use commercial_name for human-readable names.

table_columns.name: index_name
table_columns.description: Internal search key (often a username). Do NOT display this to users.

table_columns.name: commercial_name
table_columns.description: The human-readable business name, e.g. 'Smart Insurance Company', 'FREEWAY INSURANCE TX'.

sample_queries.question: List the top 5 providers by policy count
sample_queries.sql: SELECT pr.commercial_name, COUNT(p.system_id) AS policy_count FROM providers pr JOIN policies p ON pr.system_id = p.provider_ref GROUP BY pr.commercial_name ORDER BY policy_count DESC LIMIT 5

rules: ALWAYS use commercial_name for display, never index_name.
Join with policies using providers.system_id = policies.provider_ref.
Providers are Agencies. Do not confuse them with Customers.
Use LIKE for text matching on commercial_name.
